text.title: e2e-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for e2e-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: E2E tests cover complete user journeys only — not individual component behavior or API logic.
rule.rationale: E2E tests are 10-20x slower than unit tests. Testing logic already covered at cheaper layers wastes CI budget and creates maintenance cost without adding signal.

rule.statement: Limit the critical-path E2E suite to less than 50 tests. Gate non-critical tests to a nightly run.
rule.rationale: Suites beyond 50 tests routinely exceed 30 minutes in CI, causing teams to skip or disable them. A tight budget forces selection of only the most valuable journeys.

rule.statement: Use only `getByTestId()`, `getByRole()`, or `getByLabel()` locators. Never use CSS selectors, IDs, or XPath.
rule.rationale: CSS selectors break on any style refactor. `data-testid` attributes are stable and signal intent to developers that the element is tested. `getByRole` aligns tests with accessibility expectations.

rule.statement: Never use `page.waitForTimeout()` or hard-coded sleeps. Use Playwright's built-in auto-wait via `expect(locator).toBeVisible()` or explicit network/navigation waits.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded waits are the primary source of flakiness in E2E suites. Playwright auto-waits for actionability; explicit assertions wait for the right state.

rule.statement: Each test must seed its own data via API in `beforeEach` and clean up in `afterEach`. Never rely on data left by another test.
rule.rationale: Test-order dependencies cause cascading failures that are difficult to diagnose. API-based seeding with timestamped unique data prevents `unique_email` violations.

rule.statement: Reuse `storageState` for authenticated sessions — log in once in global setup; use saved cookies for the rest of the suite.
rule.rationale: Logging in via UI per test adds 2-5 seconds each. Saved auth state reduces suite time by 30-50% on auth-heavy applications.

rule.statement: Create one Page Object class per route. Name methods after user intent, not DOM events: `login()` not `clickSubmitButton()`.
rule.rationale: Intent-based names make test specs read like user stories. DOM-event names couple the test to the implementation and break on any UI restructure.

rule.statement: Create and clean up test data via API endpoints in beforeEach/afterEach, never through the UI.
rule.rationale: UI-based seeding tests something other than the actual test scenario and is 5-10x slower. API seeding is deterministic and isolated.

rule.statement: Mock all third-party API calls at the network boundary using `page.route()`. Never depend on live SaaS in CI.
rule.rationale: Real third-party calls add 1-2% flake rate from network variability. Stripe, Auth0, and similar services have sandbox modes but even those add latency and can have outages.
